      I mean to acti"""
      Utility Commands Module
      Utility slash commands for server management and user convenience.
      """

      import discord
      from discord.ext import commands
      from discord import app_commands
      import logging
      from datetime import datetime
      from config import BotConfig


      class UtilityCommands(commands.Cog):
      """Utility command implementations."""

      def __init__(self, bot: commands.Bot):
      self.bot = bot
      self.logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
      self.config = BotConfig()

      @app_commands.command(name="serverinfo", description="Get information about the current server")
      async def serverinfo(self, interaction: discord.Interaction):
      """Display detailed information about the current server."""
      if not interaction.guild:
      await interaction.response.send_message("❌ This command can only be used in a server!", ephemeral=True)
      return

      guild = interaction.guild
      embed = discord.Embed(
      title=f"🏰 {guild.name}",
      color=self.config.get_embed_color(),
      timestamp=datetime.utcnow()
      )
      embed.add_field(
      name="📊 Server Statistics",
      value=f"**Members:** {guild.member_count}\n"
      f"**Channels:** {len(guild.channels)}\n"
      f"**Roles:** {len(guild.roles)}\n"
      f"**Emojis:** {len(guild.emojis)}",
      inline=True
      )
      created_at = guild.created_at.strftime("%B %d, %Y")
      embed.add_field(
      name="ℹ️ Server Details",
      value=f"**Owner:** <@{guild.owner_id}>\n"
      f"**Created:** {created_at}\n"
      f"**Server ID:** {guild.id}\n"
      f"**Boost Level:** {guild.premium_tier}",
      inline=True
      )
      if guild.icon:
      embed.set_thumbnail(url=guild.icon.url)
      embed.set_footer(
      text=f"Requested by {interaction.user.display_name}",
      icon_url=interaction.user.display_avatar.url

      DiscordBot Source CodeFile

      Bot is back online and your zip file is ready to download (you'll see it in the chat above).

      Here's how to use it to upload to GitHub:

      1. Download the discordbot.zip file from the chat
      2. Extract/unzip it on your computer (right-click → Extract All on Windows, or double-click on Mac)
      3. Open your GitHub repo in your browser: https://github.com/Tazzy-lol/DiscordBot
      4. On the empty repo page, click "uploading an existing file" (it's a blue link in the middle of the page)
      5. Drag and drop all the extracted files/folders into the upload area
      6. Scroll down, type a commit message like Initial upload
      7. Click the green "Commit changes" button

      That's it! All your bot files will be on GitHub. The zip already excludes any sensitive data — it only has the safe stuff: your code, .env.example (template, not real token), and .gitignore.

      the file does not open

      1 minute ago

      Bot is online! Let's skip the zip approach and use Replit's Git tool directly — it's actually simpler. The Git tab is already open in your workspace.

      Here's what to do step by step:

      1. Click the "Git" tab at the top of your Replit workspace (where you saw "Settings" earlier)

      2. If you're on the Settings page, look at the very top-left of the Git panel for a back arrow ← or the word "Git" as a link — click it to go back to the main view

      3. On the main Git page, you should see:

        • A list showing your files like bot.py, main.py, commands/, etc. with checkboxes or "+" icons next to them
        • At the bottom, a text box that says something like "Commit message"

      4. Tell me what you see when you click the Git tab — describe the page or send a screenshot. I'll guide you exactly from there.

      Don't worry, we're really close. Just need to find that commit message box on the main Git page!
